param.label: K-mer size
param.help: Select the k-mer size of the signature to use. It must match the k-mer size of one or more of the input signatures. Common values: 21, 31, 51. If left blank, sourmash uses the k-mer size found in the input signatures; you only need to set it when the signatures contain more than one k-mer size.

param.name: mol_type
param.label: Molecule type
param.help: Choose the molecule/encoding for signatures to perform function on. All signatures must match this.

param.name: picklist_file
param.label: Picklist file
param.help: Input signatures files created with sourmash sketch

param.name: pickstyle
param.label: Pick style
param.help: Whether to include or exclude picked signatures. Used only when picklist file is uploaded!

macros.token:
**Picklist**
Picklist is a feature by which you can select or “pick out” signatures based on values in a CSV file. This is typically used to index, extract, or search a subset of a large collection where modifying the collection itself isn’t desired.
To use Picklist just upload a file listting only the signatures you want to include/exclude. ! Please note to chose a pickstyle **only** if a picklist file is uploaded !
You can generate the *label.csv files* using the Output parameter *output labels csv* in Sourmash compare.
or using *sourmash sig describe --csv out.csv signatures* or *sourmash sig manifest -o out.csv 'filename_or_db'* to construct an initial CSV file that you can then edit further and use as a picklist as above.

param.name: gather_csv
param.label: gather CSV
param.help: One or more CSVs from sourmash gather

param.name: taxonomy_csv
param.label: taxonomy CSV
param.help: Taxonomy File. Database lineages CSV or SQLite database

param.name: rank
param.label: Rank
param.help: Classify to this rank (tax genome) or summarize taxonomy at this rank and above (tax metagenome). Note that the taxonomy CSV must contain lineage information at this rank

param.name: taxonomy_files
param.label: taxonomy CSV Files or SQLite database
param.help: One or more properly formatted taxonomy files (CSV or SQLite). sourmash also supports LIN and ICTV lineage information in compatible taxonomy files.

macros.token:
**Taxonomy File Structure**
Taxonomy files are in csv or sqlite format and they store a mapping of the identifiers from the signature names to the standard seven NCBI/GTDB taxonomy ranks - superkingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species.
Example:
*ident,superkingdom,phylum,class,order,family,genus,species* GCF_001881345.1,d__Bacteria,p__Proteobacteria,c__Gammaproteobacteria,o__Enterobacterales,f__Enterobacteriaceae,g__Escherichia,s__Escherichia coli
